step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes a 3-phase IoT utility monitoring architecture (Ingestion, Pre-processing, Post-processing) handling 100k water sensors (27M daily events). Ingestion begins with IoT sensors sending data to API Gateway, which forwards to a Lambda (Transformer) node that normalizes protocols and splits the data into SQS FIFO queues. A separate Lambda (Pre-processor) node reads from SQS and interacts with ElastiCache (Redis) to filter events down to 40%. The filtered data goes to EC2 (Post-processor) which writes to Aurora Serverless (daily statistics) and DynamoDB (events). Finally, end users access the data via web/mobile portals through a web API layer that queries Aurora and DynamoDB.

graph.name: Vigod Aqua Water Management Platform
graph.notes: IoT platform processing 27M daily events from 100k water meters. Reduces downstream processing load by 60% using an in-memory Redis deduplication/filtering step, leading to a 25% cost reduction during scale-up.

nodes.name: Water Sensors
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: 100k IoT water meters emitting three generations of protocol variants.

nodes.name: Ingestion API Gateway
nodes.notes: First point of contact for utility telemetry data ingestion.

nodes.name: Transformer Lambda
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Streamlines multiple sensor protocols into a standard format, splitting into flow and alarm segments.

nodes.name: FIFO Queues
nodes.notes: Buffers decoupled telemetry data segments to preserve strict chronological ordering.

nodes.name: Pre-processor Lambda
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Determines in real-time which data points are useful. Drops ~60% of unnecessary idle utility records.

nodes.name: In-Memory Redis Cache
nodes.notes: Stores active state of sensors to support instant deduplication and filtering decisions in the pre-processor.

nodes.name: Post-processor Engine
nodes.notes: Runs a suite of continuous processing jobs to summarize the allowed 40% of telemetry events.

nodes.name: Aurora Serverless MySQL
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores summarized daily utility statistics, aggregates, and critical alerts.

nodes.name: Event Log Store
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores discrete source/inlet event timestamps, such as water lorry deliveries and borewell triggers.

nodes.name: Web API Portal Gateway
nodes.notes: Serves end-user consumption metrics and optimization alerts to web and mobile apps.

nodes.name: Web and Mobile Users
nodes.notes: Property managers and residents viewing water consumption dashboards and sustainability reports.

edges.notes: Pushes normalized segment payloads into SQS FIFO queues.
Closes HTTP connection back to sensor.
Queries sensor states to evaluate data relevance.
Aggregates and delivers the formatted dashboard data to the user.
